en-US.description:
Sandbox Playing Flowers is your daily botanical companion, designed to reconnect you with the natural ecosystems of your region. Every morning, wake up to a stunning, AI-generated image of a native wildflower currently blooming in your US state or Canadian province.

Built entirely offline-first for absolute privacy, the app features a fully embedded vector database of thousands of native plant species. Need to identify a flower but don't know the exact terms? Use our revolutionary Vector Vibe Search to find species using semantic concepts, colors, or feelings—all processed instantly on your device without an internet connection.

UPGRADE TO THE SPECIMEN PREMIUM PASS
Take your botanical discovery to the next level with our Premium Pass:
- Unlimited Vector Vibe Searches: Explore our massive semantic database without daily limits.
- The 7-Day Archive: Missed a day? Look back at a full week of stunning native flower generations.
- Travel Mode: Override your location to explore the native flora of any region in the US and Canada.

Re-engage with the seasonal rhythms of the natural world, one native flower at a time. Designed with love, powered by state-of-the-art on-device AI.
en-US.promoText: Now featuring the Specimen Premium Pass! Unlock unlimited Vector Vibe Search, the 7-day archive, and regional Travel Mode.
en-US.releaseNotes: Introducing the Specimen Premium Pass! You can now unlock unlimited semantic searches using our offline Vector Vibe Search. We've also added the 7-day archive and regional Travel Mode so you can explore native flowers across all supported regions.
en-US.title: Sandbox Playing Flowers
en-US.subtitle: Daily Native Flower Ritual

review.notes: The app requests location permission once on first launch to resolve the user's US state, Canadian province, or country via reverse-geocoding. The region code is cached locally and the location is never transmitted off-device. Daily flower images are pre-generated server-side via a GitHub Actions cron and served from GitHub Pages — no user data is sent with these requests. Falls back to a default bucket if permission is denied or the user is in an unsupported region.
